commit.message:
fix: preserve environment editor selection when saving a non-active environment (#57)

* Initial plan

* fix: preserve environment editor selection after saving

When saving an environment while a different one is actively selected in the
environment manager, the editor was switching back to the active collection
environment.

Root cause: `EnvironmentEditorViewModel.Receive(EnvironmentChangedMessage)` was
unconditionally overwriting `SelectedEnvironment` on every broadcast. A save
triggers a list reload in EnvironmentViewModel, which re-broadcasts the same
active-env file path. The editor incorrectly treated this as a new selection.

Fix: early-return in the handler when the active environment file path has not
changed, preserving the user's current selection.

Also adds a regression test covering this scenario.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/callsmith/callsmith/sessions/78967a6f-0a62-4007-a75e-8c3374283f20

Co-authored-by: callsmith <267777561+callsmith@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use _hasInitializedEditorSelection flag to guard env editor selection

Replace the same-path guard with a semantically correct flag that tracks whether
the initial sync of SelectedEnvironment to the active collection environment has
been completed. Once done, EnvironmentChangedMessage re-broadcasts (e.g. those
triggered by a save reload) no longer override the user's selection in the editor.

The flag resets when a new collection is opened so the initial sync happens
correctly for each collection.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/callsmith/callsmith/sessions/7ffd7798-15c1-4346-b43f-e0bb7378d26d

commit.message:
fix(monitoring): increase prometheus memory limit to fix OOMKill crash loop

Prometheus was being OOMKilled on every startup (exit code 137) while
replaying 848 accumulated WAL segments. The 256Mi limit was too low for
WAL replay on startup; the Pi 4 controller has ~1.4GB free headroom.

Increased limit 256Mi -> 512Mi and request 64Mi -> 256Mi.
Once Prometheus stays up long enough to compact the WAL, memory usage
will drop back to normal levels.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(veml6040): align init I2C writes with reference driver and add robust retry

- Match reference MicroPython VEML6040 init behavior by writing one-byte
  config values at register 0x00 (shutdown then default)
- Check writeRegister return codes in initialize() so I2C write failures are
  not silently ignored
- Track init readiness and re-initialize the singleton on subsequent API
  calls when initialization previously failed, preventing permanent stuck
  all-zero readings after transient startup bus errors

This addresses field reports of persistent RGBW=0/None/Hue=0/Sat=0 despite
working hardware by removing silent init failure paths and ensuring retries.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(veml6040): fix null colour returns in block UI

Three bugs caused classifyColor() to always return null/mismatch in the
MakeCode block interface:

1. classifyColor() returned string ('red', 'none') while the block UI
   comparison dropdown uses the ColorName enum (integers). Any 'if colour
   = red' block comparison silently evaluated to false. Changed return
   type to ColorName enum so both sides of the comparison are the same
   type.

2. Three blockId values were duplicated between class methods and their
   namespace-level wrapper functions (veml6040_classify_color,
   veml6040_ambient_light, veml6040_color_temperature). PXT/MakeCode
   misbehaves with duplicate blockIds. Removed all block annotations
   from class methods — the wrapper functions are the correct block entry
   points.

3. CONFIG register (16-bit) was written with a 1-byte data buffer instead
   of 2 bytes. The missing high byte may cause a silent I2C failure,
   leaving the sensor in shutdown. Fixed to match the VEML6030 pattern.

Also added ColorName.White and ColorName.Black enum values (appended after
None to preserve existing ordinals) since classifyColor() can detect both.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(auth): align Flutter JSON keys with backend camelCase format

The backend (ASP.NET Core) returns camelCase JSON field names by default
(accessToken, refreshToken, tokenType, expiresIn), but the Flutter
AuthTokens.fromJson was parsing snake_case keys (access_token, etc.).
This caused a null TypeError on json['access_token'] as String, which
was caught by the generic catch-all handler, surfacing as
'Ein unbekannter Fehler ist aufgetreten' on login.

Changes:
- AuthTokens.fromJson: snake_case keys -> camelCase keys
- auth_service.dart refresh request body: refresh_token -> refreshToken
- auth_notifier.dart login catch-all: log actual error for debuggability

- Native fetch (replaces @whatwg-node/fetch)

commit.message:
fix: correct fractional person-day display for small optimization budgets

The person-day result was incorrectly floored/clamped to 1.0 instead of
showing the true fractional value when the computed budget was smaller
than one person-day (28,800s).

Two root causes fixed:
1. time.ts had a private formatForBasis() that hard-coded '1.0' as the
   fallback for sub-unit values instead of computing the actual fraction.
   Removed it and replaced with calls to the shared formatDurationFromSeconds()
   from units.ts, consistent with how money.ts already worked.
2. formatDurationFromSeconds() in units.ts also returned '0.0' for
   sub-smallest-unit values. Added formatFractional() helper that uses
   enough decimal places to show at least one significant digit
   (e.g. 780s → 0.03 person-days, not 0.0).

Also updates a test that was asserting the old broken clamping behaviour.
